objective: Create a unified Agent MAXX operator control-room shell and harden the visual verification/login workflow.
changes: Added shared Agent MAXX operator navigation across dashboard, Lead Desk, Lead Acquisition, tenants, and deploy surfaces.
Added operator logout through the signed operator session API.
Normalized browser-facing API calls to trailing-slash URLs to match the Next.js routing policy.
Changed successful operator login to hard same-origin navigation so middleware sees the freshly issued session cookie.
Hardened visual inspection to wait for hydrated login controls and route navigation.
Extended operator-auth contract tests to require the shared operator shell.

verification.visual: passed: npm run verify:visual with local Next dev server and throwaway operator auth

risks: Local visual verification uses a dev server because production-mode cookies are Secure and require HTTPS.
Dashboard runtime cards show degraded/loading state without a live BFF shared secret in the local visual environment.
Real-client readiness still depends on private backend policy, rotated secrets, and HTTPS domain/tunnel.
